memory.summary: I see the scholarship is mine to fill out alone, but I'm tempted by this fun activity that needs no one. The deadline isn't until Thursday, so I can choose to play now or wait.
Working on the application now gives me breathing room for Thursday, and since I can still have fun later without losing anything, I'll keep pushing this task.
I realize the professor's assignment is gone, so I'll pivot to my scholarship instead. It feels good to have control, even if it means skipping our planned chat.
I wasted the free block on my own time, and now I'm stuck behind schedule. This guilt is eating me up, but I'll just push through to finish it tomorrow.
I feel guilty about missing the fun stream, but my priority is keeping this scholarship task moving forward to avoid stress later.
I pushed through the guilt to make real progress on my scholarship, but now I'm exhausted and still behind schedule. Tomorrow will be hard.
I finally slept through the night, but my energy is still low. The guilt of missing the stream is fading as I prioritize rest over catching up.
I'm glad I slept well, but that 1-hour push on the scholarship feels heavy; tomorrow's deadline is looming and I'm worried about burning out.
I made solid progress on the scholarship, but my energy is dipping again. I'll prioritize resting tonight so I don't burn out before tomorrow's deadline.
I feel a mix of relief and lingering anxiety; the progress is solid, but that final push left me drained despite sleeping well.
I feel a mix of relief and lingering anxiety; the progress is solid, but that final push left me drained despite sleeping well.
The scholarship application is nearly done, yet I feel drained despite the relief. My priority now is resting to protect my energy for tomorrow.

Finishing now protects the future cohesion with teammate_A and prof_assignment, preventing them from picking up my slack or having to explain why I didn't push harder. It's safer to endure the mental load alone than risk friction later.

goals.rationale: While I want to recharge via fun_now immediately after work, the lingering anxiety about the application makes that transition too sharp right now. Pushing the social obligation slightly ensures I can meet cohort_3 without feeling drained or irritable, preserving the closeness we've built.
